title: aquilaria moluccana
intro: Aquilaria moluccana, also known as the agarwood tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This tree produces a resinous heartwood when infected by a certain type of fungus, which is then used to create the highly valued and fragrant agarwood. The agarwood has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of agarwood include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues, and it can be consumed as a tea, taken as capsules, or used in aromatherapy through essential oil preparations.
